title: Gate WebSocket agentSocket assignment behind authentication handshake
description:
**Source:** External audit | **Council:** P2

**Problem:** `agentSocket = ws` is set the instant a TCP connection lands (index.js, in the `wss.on('connection')` handler), before any handshake message arrives. `sendEvent()` only checks `agentSocket?.readyState === 1` — it has no idea whether that socket authenticated. Consequences:
1. An unauthenticated client receives every broadcast event (position, inventory, health, chat) from the moment it connects
2. A second connection silently replaces `agentSocket`, and the legitimate C# host stops receiving events with no error surfaced

**Fix:** 
1. Do NOT assign `agentSocket = ws` until `isAuthenticated` is true (move assignment into the handshake message handler)
2. When a new connection arrives while `agentSocket` is already authenticated and active, close the new connection with a 409 status
3. Log a warning when an unauthenticated connection is rejected

**Note:** This is partially mitigated by Sprint 32 SEC-02 which requires handshake messages before command processing. The gap is that event broadcasting starts before auth.

**Files:** `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` (WebSocket connection handler, ~lines 95-130)

**Validation:** Connect with invalid token — events should not be received. Connect with valid token — events resume. Second connection while authenticated should be rejected.
